frameworks.name: uncertainty-driven exploration
frameworks.one_line: mental exploration during planning preferentially targets options with high epistemic uncertainty

frameworks.name: human planning algorithms
frameworks.one_line: extends exploration mechanisms from action selection to internal plan-space search

frameworks.name: tree-search planning
frameworks.one_line: planning framed as selective traversal of a branching plan space

core_question: Does mental exploration during planning preferentially target plan-space branches with high epistemic uncertainty?
key_assumptions: the space of possible plans is too vast for exhaustive search
planning and execution can be behaviorally dissociated
number of successor states indexes epistemic uncertainty
manipulations: number of potential future states available from each current state
key_findings: participants preferred to explore options with more successor states after controlling for value
uncertainty effect was larger during planning than during execution
interpretations: mental exploration is uncertainty-driven, mirroring action-level exploration
the uncertainty bias reflects a property of planning algorithms, not an intrinsic preference for uncertainty
